source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: FFT on a Heterogeneous System with a General-Purpose Map-Scan Accelerator
paper.abstract: FFT implementation on custom general-purpose Map-Scan accelerator. Kleene mathematical model foundation. FPGA simulation targeting. Linear to supralinear acceleration (0.5p to 1.5p). Throughput vs latency trade-offs.

verdict.summary: Computer architecture paper with justified formalism. FFT algorithm complexity (O(n log n)), covariance matrix notation, and MAP-SCAN cell structure descriptions are necessary for parallel algorithm analysis. Mathematical framework for transpose algorithm (Eq. steps 1-3) is appropriate for complexity analysis.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Parallel computing architecture)

### Necessity Test
- Big-O complexity notation: PASS - Standard for algorithm analysis
- Matrix notation for data organization: PASS - Required for parallel data layout
- Acceleration formulas: PASS - Necessary for performance comparison

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism for parallel computing research.

paper.title: Some Considerations about the Sensing Mechanisms and Electrical Response of Carbon Nanohorns-based Gas Sensors
paper.abstract: Carbon nanohorns sensing mechanisms for humidity, ethanol, O2, O3, NH3, H2S, H2 detection. HSAB theory application. Protonic conduction (Grotthuss mechanism). Swelling mechanism analysis.

verdict.summary: Materials science review paper. Minimal equations - primarily qualitative discussion of sensing mechanisms. HSAB theory (Lewis acid-base) explanation is standard chemistry. No formalism theater detected.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2-3 (Materials/sensor science)

### Necessity Test
- HSAB theory references: PASS - Standard chemistry framework
- Sensing mechanism descriptions: PASS - Qualitative, appropriate for review
- Resistance-humidity relationships: PASS - Basic sensor characterization

**Verdict:** Appropriate minimal formalism for materials science review.

paper.title: Real-Time Low Energy Indoor Positioning System to Efficient Use of Operating Theaters with Medical Asset and Staff Tracking
paper.abstract: BLE-based indoor positioning for hospital OR efficiency. Smart transmitters on stretchers and staff. 23.7% time savings. Gateway devices with TI-CC2541. Efficiency metrics calculation.

verdict.summary: Applied IoT system paper with practical implementation focus. Minimal formalism - pseudocode (Listing 1), efficiency metrics (planned time - busy time) are straightforward. System architecture descriptions dominate. No unnecessary mathematical decoration.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2 (Applied IoT/systems engineering)

### Necessity Test
- Pseudocode for gateway: PASS - Implementation clarity
- Efficiency formulas: PASS - Simple and practical
- No decorative mathematics detected

**Verdict:** Appropriately minimal formalism for practical systems paper.

paper.title: Editorial: Special Issue on Electronics, Microtechnologies and Materials Science
paper.abstract: Editorial for CAS 2023 conference special issue. Seven extended papers from International Semiconductor Conference. Overview of papers on RFID, IC modeling, Schottky diodes, SPR sensors, DEM controller, VO2 thin films, SPI controller.

flag_details.EDITORIAL: Not a research paper - editorial/overview of special issue. Excluded from formalism analysis.

verdict.summary: Editorial paper introducing CAS 2023 special issue. Not subject to RUIN analysis as it is meta-content describing other papers rather than original research.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** N/A (Editorial content)

### Necessity Test
N/A - Not applicable to editorial content.

**Verdict:** Editorial excluded from RUIN assessment.

paper.title: Passive Radio Frequency Identification Tag with Frequency Doubler and Energy Harvesting
paper.abstract: Passive harmonic RFID tag at 2.5/5 GHz. Schottky diode frequency doubler. Energy harvesting capability. FR-4 substrate integration. 100cm readout distance demonstrated.

verdict.summary: RF engineering paper with appropriate formalism. Friis equation (Eq. 1) for antenna gain extraction is standard. S-parameter analysis, nonlinear circuit simulations are necessary for RF design. Experimental validation with fabricated prototype.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (RF/antenna engineering)

### Necessity Test
- Friis transmission equation: PASS - Standard antenna measurement
- S-parameter notation: PASS - Required for RF characterization
- Nonlinear circuit modeling: PASS - Necessary for diode analysis

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism with experimental validation.

paper.title: On the Modelling Possibilities of Integrated Circuits Behavior Using Active Learning Principles
paper.abstract: Active learning sampling for IC metamodels. GPR and neural network regression approaches. Uncertainty-based sampling. Comparison with fixed sampling schemes. Power device validation.

verdict.summary: Applied ML for IC modeling. GPR equations (Eq. 2-6) are standard and necessary for Gaussian process methodology. Covariance matrix, kernel function definitions are intrinsic to GPR. Active learning framework is clearly presented.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2-3 (ML application to IC design)

### Necessity Test
- GPR mean/variance equations: PASS - Intrinsic to GPR methodology
- Kernel function definition: PASS - Standard for GP
- Uncertainty sampling criterion: PASS - Core to active learning

**Verdict:** Appropriate formalism for ML methodology paper.

paper.title: Accurate Numerical Methods for Modeling Forward Characteristics of High Temperature Capable Schottky Diodes
paper.abstract: Newton-Raphson and Halley methods for thermionic emission equation. SiC Schottky diode characterization. Numerical overflow robustness. High temperature operation (wide ranges).

verdict.summary: Numerical analysis paper with justified heavy formalism. Thermionic emission equations (Eq. 1-2) are the core subject. Newton-Raphson iterative formula is necessary for numerical solution. Mathematical rigor appropriate for Level 3-4 numerical methods.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3-4 (Numerical methods for device physics)

### Necessity Test
- Thermionic emission equation: PASS - Core subject of paper
- Newton-Raphson formulation: PASS - Method under analysis
- Richardson constant, barrier height: PASS - Physical parameters

**Verdict:** Formalism required and appropriate for numerical methods research.

paper.title: Theoretical Studies of Intensity and Phase Based Surface Plasmon Resonance Sensors
paper.abstract: SPR sensor numerical analysis. Reflectivity and phase vs incidence angle. BK7 and fused silica substrates. Abeles matrices method. Visible wavelength range (635-780nm).

verdict.summary: Computational optics paper. Abeles matrix method is standard for multilayer optics. Snell's law, angle relationships are basic optics. Primarily numerical results presentation with minimal unnecessary formalism.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2-3 (Computational optics)

### Necessity Test
- Abeles matrices: PASS - Standard multilayer optics method
- Angle relationships: PASS - Basic optics required
- Refractive index calculations: PASS - Standard SPR characterization

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism for optical sensor analysis.

paper.title: 8-bit Dynamic Element Matching based Feedback Controller Applied to an Instrumentation Amplifier
paper.abstract: DEM for instrumentation amplifier gain error reduction. 8-bit digital controller. 180nm CMOS implementation. Gain error reduced from 1.85% to 10s of ppm. Programmable gain 1-15.

verdict.summary: IC design paper with appropriate formalism. Gain error equations (Eq. 1-2) are necessary for DEM analysis. Resistor mismatch model is standard. System diagrams and register architecture descriptions dominate over equations.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2-3 (Analog/digital IC design)

### Necessity Test
- Gain error formula (Eq. 2): PASS - Core to DEM methodology
- Resistor mismatch model (Eq. 1): PASS - Standard IC analysis
- Register/controller architecture: PASS - Implementation description

**Verdict:** Appropriate formalism for IC design methodology.

paper.title: Microstructure of VO2 Thin Films Synthesized by Pulsed Laser Deposition
paper.abstract: PLD synthesis of VO2 thin films. Rietveld XRD analysis. AFM grain size characterization. Dislocation density vs optical band gap relationship. Resistance-temperature measurements.

verdict.summary: Materials science characterization paper. Standard XRD analysis equations (Eq. 1-2 for thickness, dislocation density). Tauc plot for band gap (Eq. 3) is standard optical characterization. Primarily experimental results with minimal formalism.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2-3 (Materials characterization)

### Necessity Test
- XRR thickness formula (Eq. 1): PASS - Standard characterization
- Williamson-Smallman equation: PASS - Standard dislocation analysis
- Tauc relation (Eq. 3): PASS - Standard optical band gap method

**Verdict:** Appropriate formalism for materials science characterization.

paper.title: Improved Serial Peripheral Interface Controller Based on Scan Architecture
paper.abstract: High-frequency SPI controller with scan testability. 65nm CMOS implementation. 55MHz operation. Design for testability methodology. Defect coverage 98.54%.

verdict.summary: Digital IC design paper with minimal formalism. Architecture diagrams and implementation details dominate. No unnecessary mathematical decoration - practical design methodology focus. Test coverage metrics are standard DFT analysis.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2 (Digital IC design/DFT)

### Necessity Test
- Block diagrams: PASS - Required for architecture description
- Coverage metrics: PASS - Standard DFT analysis
- No decorative equations detected

**Verdict:** Appropriately minimal formalism for practical design paper.

batch_notes: Volume 27 Issue 2 (2024) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **No formalism theater detected.** This issue includes a CAS 2023 special issue with 7 extended conference papers (papers 770-776) plus 3 regular papers (766-768). One editorial (769) excluded from scoring. Diverse topics: parallel FFT architecture, carbon nanohorn sensors, BLE indoor positioning, RFID tags, ML for IC modeling, numerical methods for Schottky diodes, SPR sensors, DEM amplifiers, VO2 thin films, SPI controllers. All formalism is domain-appropriate with no decorative mathematical content. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 8 ADEQUATE, 2 STRONG, 1 EDITORIAL.
